Conducting both an abdominal ultrasound and a Dimercaptosuccinic Acid (DMSA) scan provides a more complete assessment of any structural abnormalities or renal function impairment which could be contributing factors to the UTI. While doing nothing would seem straightforward after resolving acute symptoms, these additional steps help identify and manage potential risk factors proactively, potentially preventing future infections and long-term kidney problems.
